summary: Joe Reis reflects on the end of 2023 and shares his philosophy on predictions and resolutions. He argues that unexpected events trump linear predictions (citing ChatGPT disrupting the Modern Data Stack narrative) and advocates for consistent daily habits over annual resolutions.
key_points: Predictions are mostly linear extrapolations that fail when unexpected events occur
ChatGPT's emergence in late 2022 completely disrupted data industry trends like Modern Data Stack and Data Mesh
Daily habits compound over years and are more effective than annual resolutions
Goals can emerge any time - they're not exclusive to New Year's
money_quotes: Unexpected events trump linear incrementalism. By definition, you can't predict these unexpected events.
Most predictions I see are linear and incremental extrapolations of the present into the future. That works until it doesn't.

In January 2022, it would be easy to assume the Modern Data Stack and Data Mesh would still reign supreme in 2023. Then in November 2022, ChatGPT ushered in the 'year of AI' in 2023, where every modern data stack vendor is suddenly an AI company.
I find that if I can improve the foundation of my mind and health and bring my best self to each day, it's much easier to execute goals, no matter when they pop up or what they are.
key_insight: Rather than making predictions or resolutions, focus on building strong daily habits that create a foundation for executing whatever goals emerge throughout the year.
